tweets.text:
Context Engineering for Agents
Agents need context to perform tasks. Context engineering is the art and science of filling the context window with just the right information at each step of an agent’s trajectory. 
https://t.co/a1FNykpzEK https://t.co/g34MAt7FKn

tweets.text:
Context Engineering 

What is it?
•⁠  ⁠Managing context windows of AI agents
•⁠  ⁠Essential skill for building reliable agents
•⁠  ⁠Practice and focus of managing what goes into context window
•⁠  ⁠Systematic approach ensuring agent has right information at right time

tweets.name: David

tweets.text:
"just add more context to the prompt" is the "just add more RAM" of AI engineering.

i built a 7-agent system at work. agents kept forgetting things between sessions.

first fix: dump everything into context. slower, more expensive, worse answers.

actual fix: structured memory
tweets.name: Hrushikesh Yadav

tweets.text:
AI agents are impressive… until they hit unreliable data.

The real bottleneck in AI right now isn’t intelligence.
It’s data quality.

Bad context = bad outputs

That’s why data engineering is becoming even more valuable in the AI era.

#AI #DataEngineering #LLM

tweets.text:
The hardest part of building agents is still context management.

What do you keep, drop, summarize, or compact when the window fills up?

Most teams won’t solve this from scratch.

The real work is building on top of solid harnesses like @droid.

That’s Harness Engineering.
